verdict: Worth doing, with one scope cut. The complexity ts-fx flags is real but moderate (validateConfig 26/39, loadConfigFile 19/33) — these are the lowest-cog hotspots in the actionable-high list, so this theme's primary value is correctness/contract cleanup (G3/G4/G6 + VERSION drift), not raw complexity reduction. Decomposing validateConfig into schema-driven per-section validators is a clean, safe win that ALSO closes the http/pyodide/per-module validation gap (G6) in one move. Making per-module `runtime` optional/advisory is correct and low-risk (it is genuinely dead input — confirmed never read by generate()). Single-sourcing VERSION and unifying timeout naming are trivial and high-credibility. The loadConfigFile decomposition is worthwhile to extract the ext-dispatch into a table, but the .cts/.ts transpile branch is intrinsically complex and verified-correct — I recommend extracting it into a named helper untouched rather than rewriting it. SCOPE CUT: do not attempt to make `runtime` drive bridge selection (no AutoBridge exists); just demote the field.
verifiedSignals: validateConfig cyclomatic/cognitive 26/39 confirmed: monolithic single function spans src/config/index.ts:120-209 with 6 inline section blocks (top-level allowlist, output, pythonImportPath, runtime.node, performance, types) and ~15 throw sites

loadConfigFile 19/33 confirmed: src/config/index.ts:259-387 is a linear ext-dispatch (.json/.cjs/.js+.mjs/.ts+.mts+.cts/throw) with the .ts/.cts branch (291-384) carrying the bulk of the cognitive load (emitCommonJs split, in-memory Module._compile path, temp .mjs ESM path)

createRequire false positives CONFIRMED correct, do NOT touch: src/config/index.ts:278 (createRequire for .cjs) and :326 (createRequire('module') for in-memory .cts compile) both use createRequire(import.meta.url) — valid ESM idiom

Per-module runtime field ignored CONFIRMED (G3): PythonModuleConfig.runtime is REQUIRED at src/types/index.ts:313; generate() in src/tywrap.ts only ever reads moduleConfig.exclude(:207), .excludePatterns(:209), .functions(:233), .classes(:249), .version(:625 cache key), .typeHints(:641 cache key) — grep of moduleConfig.* shows NO .runtime read
CLI writes the dead field: src/cli.ts:53 buildModulesConfig emits {runtime, typeHints:'strict'}, cli.ts:75 emits it into generated init config text, cli.ts:459 passes argv.runtime
VERSION drift CONFIRMED: src/index.ts:150 export const VERSION = '0.3.0' vs package.json:3 version 0.4.0

timeout/timeoutMs mismatch CONFIRMED (G4): config NodeConfig.timeout at src/types/index.ts:346 is mapped to bridge timeoutMs at src/tywrap.ts:172 (fetchPythonIr) and node.ts uses timeoutMs at src/runtime/node.ts:58/335

validateConfig skips http/pyodide/per-module CONFIRMED (G6): src/config/index.ts:157-167 validates only runtime.node; runtime.http and runtime.pyodide are never inspected; pythonModules entries are never validated (only legacy 'watch' key detected in detectLegacyFields)

No AutoBridge: grep confirms 'auto' appears only as a RuntimeStrategy choice in cli.ts:161/414, the type union types/index.ts:295, and a hardcoded GenerationMetadata.runtime:'auto' at src/core/generator.ts:1221 — no bridge consumes 'auto'
resolveJsonModule already enabled at tsconfig.json:31, so import pkg from '../../package.json' with {type:'json'} is viable for single-sourcing VERSION
falsePositives: ts-fx verify-esm require flags at config/index.ts:280/327 (1-indexed in digest; actual createRequire calls at :278 and :326) are FALSE POSITIVES — they use createRequire(import.meta.url), the supported ESM idiom for loading .cjs configs and Node's private Module API for in-memory .cts compile. Confirmed by reading the source; must not be 'fixed'.

REVIEW.md G3 says 'runtime field at types/index.ts:313 required' — the line is correct but note the field is required at the TYPE level only; at runtime createConfig/validateConfig never enforces or reads it, so removing/optionalizing it is purely a type+CLI change with zero runtime-behavior coupling (slightly less severe than 'required' implies).

REVIEW.md G4 frames timeout-vs-timeoutMs as a 'mismatch' bug — it is actually already correctly bridged at tywrap.ts:172 (config.runtime.node.timeout -> fetchPythonIr timeoutMs). The real issue is naming inconsistency/DX, not a functional defect. Scope this as a rename/alias, not a fix.
proposal:
Restructure src/config/index.ts around a small declarative section-validator registry plus an extracted loader-dispatch table, and remove the dead/duplicated config contract surface.

1) VALIDATION (decompose validateConfig). Replace the monolithic validateConfig with a list of named per-section validators invoked in sequence: validateTopLevelKeys(config), validateOutput(config.output), validatePythonImportPath, validateRuntime(config.runtime) [now covering node + http + pyodide], validatePerformance, validateTypes, validateDebug, validatePythonModules(config.pythonModules). Each is a small pure function (cyc<6) taking its slice and throwing user-facing errors. Drive the primitive checks from a tiny schema helper: a `Field` descriptor { path, check: (v)=>boolean, message } and an `assertField` runner, so the repetitive 'typeof x must be string/boolean/number>=0/enum' blocks collapse into table rows. Keep detectLegacyFields as the first call. NEW coverage: validateRuntime checks runtime.http.baseURL is a non-empty string parseable as a URL and runtime.http.timeout/runtime.node.timeout/runtime.pyodide as appropriate; validatePythonModules iterates entries asserting typeHints in {strict,loose,ignore}, optional functions/classes/exclude/excludePatterns are string[], alias is string.

2) LOADING (simplify ext-dispatch). Replace the if-ladder in loadConfigFile with a lookup keyed by normalized extension: loaders = { '.json': loadJsonConfig, '.cjs': loadCjsConfig, '.js': loadEsmConfig, '.mjs': loadEsmConfig, '.ts': loadTsConfig, '.mts': loadTsConfig, '.cts': loadTsConfig }. loadConfigFile becomes: normalize ext -> existence check -> dispatch -> ensureConfigObject, throwing 'Unsupported extension' for misses. Extract the existing TS branch verbatim into loadTsConfig(resolved, ext) and the emitCommonJs path into a private loadCtsInMemory(transpiled, resolved) helper WITHOUT altering the createRequire/Module._compile logic (verified correct). This drops loadConfigFile's own cog from ~33 to <10 while leaving the genuinely-complex transpile code intact and individually named.

3) CONTRACT: make PythonModuleConfig.runtime optional and document it as advisory/unused-by-generate (or remove it entirely — preferred for pre-1.0). Stop emitting `runtime:` in CLI init/generate output (cli.ts:53,75) and in buildModulesConfig. Keep the CLI `--runtime` flag only if it still feeds top-level runtime overrides (cli.ts:257); otherwise drop it. Remove 'auto' from RuntimeStrategy or document it as a no-op CLI alias (no AutoBridge exists).

4) VERSION: replace `export const VERSION='0.3.0'` (index.ts:150) with `import pkg from '../package.json' with { type: 'json' }; export const VERSION = pkg.version;` (resolveJsonModule already on). Add a CI/test drift assertion comparing VERSION to package.json.

5) TIMEOUT: standardize on config `timeout` (ms) as the documented config name; add a JSDoc on NodeConfig.timeout/HttpConfig.timeout noting it maps to the bridge timeoutMs, OR rename config fields to timeoutMs for symmetry. Pick ONE and document the mapping at the single conversion site (tywrap.ts:172).
breakingChanges: PythonModuleConfig.runtime changes from required to optional (or is removed). Configs that TypeScript-required this field still compile; runtime behavior unchanged since it was never read. If removed entirely, configs/tests that set it must drop it (test/cli.test.ts, test/integration.test.ts, generated init output, examples).

CLI `tywrap init` / `tywrap generate` will no longer emit `runtime: '<x>'` into per-module config objects (cli.ts:53,75). Generated config snapshots change (test/generated_snapshot.test.ts may need updating).
If 'auto' is removed from RuntimeStrategy, any config/type referencing 'auto' as a per-module runtime fails to type-check. Mitigation: keep it as a documented no-op alias instead of removing.

Stricter config validation now REJECTS previously-silently-accepted invalid input: bad runtime.http.baseURL (non-URL), invalid per-module typeHints values, non-string functions/classes/exclude arrays, and unknown per-module keys (if enforced). Configs that were 'working' only because validation skipped these sections will now throw at load time. This is intentional (G6) but is a behavior change that must be in the migration guide.
VERSION export value changes from '0.3.0' to the live package.json version (0.4.0+). Anything asserting VERSION==='0.3.0' breaks (intended).

If config field is renamed timeout->timeoutMs (option B of step 5), existing configs using `timeout` break and need migration. If kept as timeout (option A), no break — recommend option A to avoid the break.
verificationPlan: Behavior-preserving for the loader; intentionally behavior-changing (stricter) for validation. (1) Run existing test/config_loading.test.ts and test/runtime_config.test.ts unchanged — the loader refactor must keep them green (proves .ts/.cts/.json/.cjs/.mjs dispatch + temp-file cleanup + read-only-dir paths still work). (2) Add characterization tests for validateConfig BEFORE refactoring: capture current accept/reject behavior for each section, then assert the decomposed validators reproduce it (plus the NEW rejections for http.baseURL/per-module/typeHints as added positive failure cases). (3) Add a VERSION drift test asserting VERSION===package.json version. (4) Add tests that a config with per-module runtime omitted now type-checks and generates identically (golden output unchanged), and one with runtime present is accepted (advisory). (5) Re-run ts-fx complexity scan; confirm validateConfig and loadConfigFile drop out of the actionable-high list and no new function inherits >cog 30. (6) npm run check:all (format, lint, build, type tests, unit) green. (7) grep to confirm createRequire lines unchanged byte-for-byte.
steps: Add characterization tests in test/config_validation.test.ts capturing current validateConfig accept/reject outcomes per section (lock behavior before touching it).
Add VERSION drift test, then change src/index.ts:150 to import package.json (with { type: 'json' }) and export pkg.version; verify resolveJsonModule path compiles for both dist and tools tsconfigs.

Introduce a Field/assertField schema helper and refactor validateConfig into validateTopLevelKeys, validateOutput, validatePythonImportPath, validateRuntime, validatePerformance, validateTypes, validateDebug, validatePythonModules; keep detectLegacyFields first. Each section <cyc 6.

Extend validateRuntime to cover runtime.http (baseURL non-empty + URL-parseable, timeout number>=0, headers object) and runtime.pyodide (indexURL string, packages string[]); add validatePythonModules enforcing typeHints enum + optional string[] fields. Wire the new positive-failure cases into the characterization tests.

Refactor loadConfigFile: build a const loaders dispatch table keyed by extension; extract loadJsonConfig, loadCjsConfig, loadEsmConfig, loadTsConfig (and private loadCtsInMemory) moving the existing transpile/createRequire/Module._compile code VERBATIM into loadTsConfig/loadCtsInMemory. Reduce loadConfigFile to normalize+exist-check+dispatch.
Confirm via grep/diff that the createRequire(import.meta.url) calls and Module._compile logic are byte-identical after extraction.

Make PythonModuleConfig.runtime optional in src/types/index.ts (or remove it); add JSDoc noting it is advisory and not consumed by generate(). Decide 'auto' fate (recommend: keep as documented no-op alias).

Update CLI: stop emitting per-module runtime in buildModulesConfig and the init template text (cli.ts:53,75); keep --runtime only if it feeds top-level runtime override (cli.ts:257), else drop the flag and its choices.
Add JSDoc to NodeConfig.timeout / HttpConfig.timeout documenting the ms unit and the single mapping to bridge timeoutMs at tywrap.ts:172 (option A: keep name 'timeout', no break).

Update affected tests/snapshots (cli.test.ts, integration.test.ts, generated_snapshot.test.ts) to drop the now-absent per-module runtime; run npm run check:all and re-run ts-fx complexity to confirm both functions exit the hotspot list.
Add a migration-guide stub enumerating: optional/removed per-module runtime, stricter validation rejections, VERSION sourcing, and the documented timeout->timeoutMs mapping (feeds G7).
